heading: State All Payer Claims Databases
label: 247d–11 State All Payer Claims Databases
notes: Codification, which directed that section 320B (this section) be added at the end of part B of title III of the Public Health Service Act, was executed as directed, notwithstanding that section 320 of the Public Health Service Act () appears in part C of title III of the Act, resulting in section 320B of the Act preceding section 320.Section 115(a) of div. BB of Pub. L. 116–260section 247e of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make one-time grants to eligible States for the purposes described in subsection (b).
To establish a State All Payer Claims Database.
To improve an existing State All Payer Claims Databases.11So in original.1

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: c Eligibility
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under subsection (a), a State shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary specifies, including, with respect to a State All Payer Claims Database, at least specifics on how the State will ensure uniform data collection and the privacy and security of such data.

children.heading: Grant period and amount
children.label: d Grant period and amount
children.text: Grants awarded under this section shall be for a period of 3-years, and in an amount of $2,500,000, of which $1,000,000 shall be made available to the State for each of the first 2 years of the grant period, and $500,000 shall be made available to the State for the third year of the grant period.2So in original. Probably should be “3 years,”.2

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the uses and methodologies for evaluating health system performance using such data; and

children.label: ii
children.text: documentation of approval of the research by an institutional review board, if applicable for a particular plan of research; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an entity such as an employer, health insurance issuer, third-party administrator, or health care provider, requesting access for the purpose of quality improvement or cost-containment, a description of the intended uses for such data.

children.heading: Access for research purposes
children.label: A Access for research purposes
children.text: Upon approval of an application for research purposes under paragraph (1)(A), the authorized user shall enter into a data use and confidentiality agreement with the State All Payer Claims Database that has received a grant under this subsection, which shall include a prohibition on attempts to reidentify and disclose individually identifiable health information and proprietary financial information.

children.heading: Customized reports
children.label: B Customized reports
children.text: Employers and employer organizations may request customized reports from a State All Payer Claims Database that has received a grant under this section, at cost, subject to the requirements of this section with respect to privacy, security, and proprietary financial information.

children.heading: Non-customized reports
children.label: C Non-customized reports
children.text: A State All Payer Claims Database that has received a grant under this section shall make available to all authorized users aggregate data sets available through the State All Payer Claims Database, free of charge.

children.heading: Waivers
children.label: 3 Waivers
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirements of this subsection of a State All Payer Claims Database to provide access of entities to such database if such State All Payer Claims Database is substantially in compliance with this subsection.

children.heading: Multi-State applications
children.label: 1 Multi-State applications
children.text: The Secretary may prioritize applications submitted by a State whose application demonstrates that the State will work with other State All Payer Claims Databases to establish a single application for access to data by authorized users across multiple States.

children.heading: Expansion of data sets
children.label: 2 Expansion of data sets
children.text: The Secretary may prioritize applications submitted by a State whose application demonstrates that the State will implement the reporting format for self-insured group health plans described in .section 1191d of title 29
the term “individually identifiable health information” has the meaning given such term in ;section 1320d(6) of this title

the term “proprietary financial information” means data that would disclose the terms of a specific contract between an individual health care provider or facility and a specific group health plan, managed care entity (as defined in ) or other managed care organization, or health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage; andsection 1396u–2(a)(1)(B) of this title

the term “State All Payer Claims Database” means, with respect to a State, a database that may include medical claims, pharmacy claims, dental claims, and eligibility and provider files, which are collected from private and public payers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 and 2023, and $25,000,000 for fiscal year 2024, to remain available until expended.
